meanings.definition: supplement what is thought to be deficient
examples: He eked out his meager pay by giving private lessons
Braque eked out his collages with charcoal

meanings.definition: live from day to day, as with some hardship
examples: He eked out his years in great poverty

meanings.definition: make by laborious and precarious means
examples: He eked out a living as a painter
He eked out some information from the archives
